key_themes: Strong analyst consensus with Buy/Strong Buy ratings and 20-25% upside to price targets (~$338-350)
Consistent earnings beats and projected Q1 2026 EPS growth of ~12% (expected ~$7.57-8.08)
Attractive valuation after recent share price weakness, viewed as undervalued by value investors
PBM transformation, FTC settlement, and improved medical cost outlook for 2026
Operational strengths including JD Power digital experience recognition and community health initiatives
summary: Financial sentiment for Cigna Group (CI) is solidly positive as of mid-April 2026. Wall Street analysts maintain strong buy ratings with significant upside from the current ~$276-278 share price, supported by a history of earnings beats, projected EPS growth, and strategic progress in its pharmacy benefit management business following the FTC settlement.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CI/)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/healthcare/articles/heres-expect-cigna-groups-next-131631057.html)[[3]](https://tickernerd.com/stock/ci-forecast/) Value investors on Reddit frequently describe it as undervalued with strong long-term compounding characteristics, though some caution exists around potential PBM regulatory changes and broader managed care sector pressures. Recent positive news on digital experience awards and dividend growth reinforces the constructive outlook ahead of Q1 2026 earnings on April 30.[[4]](https://www.reddit.com/r/ValueInvesting/comments/153j8rz/what_am_i_missing_with_cigna/)[[5]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CI/press-releases/)
sources: 25+ recent news articles and analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, Reuters, MarketBeat, Barchart), ~20 X posts reviewed (predominantly low-engagement; limited viral discussion on $CI), Multiple positive Reddit threads in r/ValueInvesting and r/stocks highlighting undervaluation
